source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure AWS Backup with Service Linked Roles

input_row.source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure AWS Backup with Service Linked Roles
input_row.description: AWS Service Linked Roles are IAM roles designed specifically for AWS Backup. These roles come with default configurations allowing access to all AWS resources by default.
input_row.rationale: While Service Linked Roles offer quick deployment, using default configurations isn't recommended for security best practices.
input_row.audit: Create service-linked role for AWS Backup: You don't need to create a service-linked role manually. AWS Backup automatically creates it when you list resources to back up, set up cross-account backup, or perform backups using the AWS Management Console, AWS CLI, or AWS API. If you delete this role, you can recreate it by following the same steps. AWS Backup will create it for you again when needed.
input_row.remediation: Assess your organization's needs to determine whether to utilize Service Linked Roles for AWS backups. References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/aws-backup/latest/devguide/using-service-linked- roles.html 2 Elastic Block Store (EBS) Amazon EBS is a block level file storage system that runs on EC2. EBS can be used as a hard drive that’s mounted on an EC2 instance (virtual machine). EBS can store data as a standalone apart from EC2. This means that data can persist to the block storage service while an EC2 instance is offline. Out of the box EBS functions as an unformatted file system that needs to be configured and mounted on top of an EC2 instance. EBS can be used as both a storage and boot drive; for the purposes of this document, we will focus on EBS as a storage device. EBS comes with many different options to fit the specific needs of an application. EBS is most likely the right choice if you need quick access to read and write files to the cloud and you need these files to be stored long term. You can also rapidly unmount the file system from one EC2 instance and deploy it to another instance by using snapshots.
input_row.references: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/aws-backup/latest/devguide/using-service-linked- roles.html 2 Elastic Block Store (EBS) Amazon EBS is a block level file storage system that runs on EC2. EBS can be used as a hard drive that’s mounted on an EC2 instance (virtual machine). EBS can store data as a standalone apart from EC2. step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- ✓ Can AWS APIs query this property? → Automated
- ✓ Is this a standard AWS service property (encryption, logging, access control, network rules)? → Automated
- ✓ Does boto3 SDK have methods to check this? → Automated
- ✓ Does AWS CLI have commands to retrieve this? → Automated
- ✓ Is it about IAM, S3, EC2, VPC, CloudTrail, Config, KMS, etc.? → Automated (AWS APIs expose these)

Given that AWS Backup with Service Linked Roles can be programmatically verified, the control is suitable for an automated approach. The audit process involves checking if the automatically created service-linked role for AWS Backup exists, which can be accomplished through AWS IAM APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_backup_service_linked_role_exists

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **AWS API calls or boto3 SDK methods:** 
  - Use the `boto3` client for IAM to list roles and check for the existence of the AWS Backup service-linked role.
  - Specifically, use `iam.list_roles()` to list all roles and parse the results to confirm the role:
    - Role Name: `AWSServiceRoleForBackup`.
  
- **AWS CLI commands (if applicable):**
  - Use the command `aws iam list-roles --query "Roles[?RoleName=='AWSServiceRoleForBackup'] | length(@)"` to count the number of roles with the name `AWSServiceRoleForBackup`.

- **Specific resources to enumerate:** 
  - The IAM roles in the current AWS account.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Ensure the role named `AWSServiceRoleForBackup` exists and has an attached policy that grants appropriate permissions required by AWS Backup.
  
- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** The AWS Backup service-linked role (`AWSServiceRoleForBackup`) exists and is active.
  - **Fail:** The AWS Backup service-linked role does not exist, which means it might have been deleted or was never created by any AWS Backup operation.

In summary, the control can be efficiently audited using automated methods by verifying the existence and configuration of the designated service-linked role through AWS IAM.
